message:
feat(robot): rewrite --robot-wait with split pane/attention condition evaluation

Major refactor of the wait system to properly support mixed pane-state
and attention-feed conditions:

Architecture (wait.go):
- splitWaitConditions() separates comma-delimited conditions into
  paneConditions and attentionConditions
- Mixed waits are now ANDed across both surfaces (previously attention
  was checked first and returned immediately, ignoring pane conditions)
- Pane polling skipped entirely for pure attention waits (no tmux calls)
- Timeout response includes CursorInfo when attention was tracked
- Cursor expiration handled explicitly: CursorExpiredError returns
  exit code 2 with ErrCodeCursorExpired and resync command
- checkAttentionConditions() rewritten: ANDs all conditions (every
  condition must match) instead of returning on first match
- Returns composite result with matched_conditions and
  match_count_by_condition
- checkSingleAttentionCondition() returns lightweight match struct
- New attentionEventMatchesWaitCondition() pure predicate using
  deriveAttentionSignal() and attentionActionabilityRank()
- New helpers: attentionEventSignal(), buildWaitWakePayload(),
  buildWaitCursorInfo(), newAttentionConditionResult()

Tests (wait_test.go):
- TestSplitWaitConditions: pane/attention splitting with whitespace trim
- TestAttentionEventMatchesWaitCondition: 8-condition table-driven test
- TestCheckAttentionConditions_AllConditionsRequired: AND semantics
  with match counts and session filtering
- TestCheckAttentionConditions_CursorExpired: expired cursor handling

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
